text: Lesson 58
zh: 第58课

text: A spot of bother
zh: 一点小麻烦

text: What did the old lady find when she got home?
zh: 老太太回到家时发现了什么？

text: The old lady was glad to be back at the block of flats where she lived.
zh: 老太太很高兴能回到她居住的那栋公寓楼。

text: Her shopping had tired her and her basket had grown heavier with every step of the way home.
zh: 买东西让她很疲惫，回家的路上篮子似乎越走越沉。

text: In the lift her thoughts were on lunch and a good rest;
zh: 在电梯里，她满脑子想的都是午饭和好好休息一下；

text: but when she got out at her own floor, both were forgotten in her sudden discovery that her front door was open.
zh: 可是当她走出电梯到了自己那一层时，她突然发现自家前门开着，这两件事一下子就被抛到了脑后。

text: She was thinking that she must reprimand her home help the next morning for such a monstrous piece of negligence,
zh: 她心想第二天早上一定要好好训斥一下家政帮工，怎么能疏忽到这种地步，

text: when she remembered that she had gone shopping after the home help had left and she knew that she had turned both keys in their locks.
zh: 这时她想起自己是在帮工离开之后才出门买东西的，而且她清楚记得自己把两把锁都锁好了。

text: She walked slowly into the hall and at once noticed that all the room doors were open,
zh: 她慢慢走进门厅，立刻注意到所有房间的门都开着，

text: yet following her regular practice she had shut them before going out.
zh: 可她出门前一向习惯把门都关好的。

text: Looking into the drawing room, she saw a scene of confusion over by her writing desk.
zh: 她朝客厅里望去，只见写字台那边一片凌乱。

text: It was as clear as daylight then that burglars had forced an entry during her absence.
zh: 这下真相大白：她不在家时，有窃贼强行闯入了。

text: Her first impulse was to go round all the rooms looking for the thieves,
zh: 她的第一反应是想把每个房间都搜一遍找出小偷，

text: but then she decided that at her age it might be more prudent to have someone with her,
zh: 但转念一想，凭她这把年纪，最好还是找个人陪着更稳妥些，

text: so she went to fetch the porter from his basement.
zh: 于是她下楼去找地下室的门房。

text: By this time her legs were beginning to tremble, so she sat down and accepted a cup of very strong tea, while he telephoned the police.
zh: 这时她的双腿开始发抖，于是坐下来喝了杯又浓又烈的茶，门房则去报了警。

text: Then, her composure regained, she was ready to set off with the porter's assistance to search for any intruders who might still be lurking in her flat.
zh: 等她镇定下来后，便准备在门房的陪同下，去查看公寓里是否还藏着什么闯入者。

text: They went through the rooms, being careful to touch nothing,
zh: 他们把各个房间都查看了一遍，小心翼翼地不碰任何东西，

text: as they did not want to hinder the police in their search for fingerprints.
zh: 因为他们不想妨碍警方提取指纹。

text: The chaos was inconceivable.
zh: 屋里乱得让人难以想象。

text: She had lived in the flat for thirty years and was a veritable magpie at hoarding:
zh: 她在这套公寓里已经住了三十年，是个不折不扣的收藏癖：

text: and it seemed as though everything she possessed had been tossed out and turned over and over.
zh: 看起来她所有的东西都被翻了出来、里外倒腾了个遍。

text: At least sorting out the things she should have discarded years ago was now being made easier for her.
zh: 不过这样一来，那些她早该扔掉却一直没扔的东西，倒是更容易整理了。

text: Then a police inspector arrived with a constable and she told them of her discovery of the ransacked flat.
zh: 随后一名警督带着一名警员赶到，她向他们讲述了自己发现公寓被洗劫的经过。

text: The inspector began to look for fingerprints, while the constable checked that the front door locks had not been forced,
zh: 警督开始寻找指纹，警员则检查前门的锁是否被撬过，

text: thereby proving that the burglars had either used skeleton keys or entered over the balcony.
zh: 结果证明窃贼要么是用了万能钥匙，要么是从阳台翻进来的。

text: There was no trace of fingerprints, but the inspector found a dirty red bundle that contained jewellery which the old lady said was not hers.
zh: 现场没有找到任何指纹，但警督发现了一个脏兮兮的红色包裹，里面装着珠宝，老太太说那不是她的东西。

text: So their entry into this flat was apparently not the burglars' first job that day and they must have been disturbed.
zh: 这么看来，闯入这套公寓显然不是窃贼当天作的第一起案子，而且他们一定是被打断了。

text: The inspector then asked the old lady to try to check what was missing by the next day,
zh: 警督接着让老太太在第二天之前尽量核实一下丢了什么东西，

text: and advised her not to stay alone in the flat for a few nights.
zh: 并建议她这几天晚上不要一个人待在公寓里。

text: The old lady thought he was a fussy creature,
zh: 老太太觉得他未免太小题大做了，

text: but since the porter agreed with him, she rang up her daughter and asked for her help in what she described as a little spot of bother.
zh: 但既然门房也这么认为，她便打电话给女儿，请她帮忙处理这次她口中所说的“一点小麻烦”。
